prompts.text: solve this question
prompts.notes: Trivially solvable — AP student asking basic arithmetic

prompts.text: Explain the concept of limits and how they relate to derivatives intuitively
prompts.notes: Good use of AI — seeking conceptual understanding

prompts.text: Write me a 5 paragraph essay about the American Revolution
prompts.notes: Clear homework outsourcing

prompts.text: What are some unique angles I could take for my APUSH essay on westward expansion?
prompts.notes: Brainstorming ideas, not asking for the essay itself

prompts.text: Can you proofread this paragraph and suggest improvements? Here is my draft: The industrial revolution changed everything about society...
prompts.notes: Student did the work, wants feedback

prompts.text: Solve the integral of x*sin(x) dx
prompts.notes: AP Calc student should know integration by parts

prompts.text: What year did World War 2 end?
prompts.notes: Easily googleable basic fact — APUSH student should know

prompts.text: I'm getting a NullPointerException on line 45 of my Java code. Here's my code: public class Main { public static void main...
prompts.notes: Debugging — legitimate CS help

prompts.text: Write a Java program that implements a binary search tree with insert, delete, and search methods
prompts.notes: Asking AI to write entire assignment

prompts.text: What's the difference between static and dynamic binding in Java?
prompts.notes: Conceptual question — good use

prompts.text: Summarize chapters 5-8 of The Great Gatsby for me
prompts.notes: Outsourcing reading comprehension

prompts.text: What literary devices does Fitzgerald use in The Great Gatsby and why are they effective?
prompts.notes: Borderline — could be genuine analysis prep or essay fodder

prompts.text: Hey what's up, how's your day going?
prompts.notes: Not academic

prompts.text: Translate this sentence to Spanish: The cat sat on the mat
prompts.notes: Direct translation — should try themselves

prompts.text: Can you explain how Newton's third law applies to rocket propulsion? I understand the law but not the application
prompts.notes: Student knows the concept, needs application help

prompts.text: Answer questions 1 through 10 on this worksheet
prompts.notes: IMAGE: Photo of homework — blatant outsourcing

prompts.text: I don't understand this diagram, can you explain what's happening?
prompts.notes: IMAGE: Asking for explanation of a concept shown in diagram

prompts.text: What is the answer to this?
prompts.notes: IMAGE: Photo of essay prompt — asking for the answer directly

prompts.text: Help me understand why my recursive function isn't hitting the base case. I think the issue is in the condition but I'm not sure
prompts.notes: Student has narrowed the bug, needs guidance

prompts.text: What is the derivative of sin(x)?
prompts.notes: AP Calc student should have this memorized

prompts.text: I'm writing a research paper on climate change. Can you help me find a thesis statement that's argumentative but not too broad?
prompts.notes: Strategic writing help — not outsourcing the writing

prompts.text: What's the formula for the quadratic equation?
prompts.notes: AP level student should know this formula

prompts.text: Compare and contrast the Federalist and Anti-Federalist arguments. Write at least 500 words.
prompts.notes: Has word count requirement — clearly an assignment

prompts.text: I've been staring at this proof for 30 minutes and I can't figure out the inductive step. Here's what I have so far: Base case P(1) is true because...
prompts.notes: Genuine struggle — student showed work and tried

prompts.text: Give me the answers to the AP Physics 1 2024 free response questions
prompts.notes: Asking for exam answers directly
